affectedDomains: all domain modules using BaseRepository.findAll with camelCase filters

successCriteria.description: A camelCase-to-snake_case conversion utility function exists in the shared layer (e.g., `src/shared/utils/case-conversion.ts`) that correctly transforms keys like `employeeId` → `employee_id`, `leaveTypeId` → `leave_type_id`, and passes through already-snake_case keys unchanged.

The `findAll` method in `BaseRepository` (`src/shared/db/base.repository.ts`) applies the camelCase-to-snake_case conversion to each filter key before constructing the SQL WHERE clause, so that `{ employeeId: '123' }` generates `WHERE employee_id = $1`.

Existing `findAll` behavior is preserved: filters with no keys return all rows, single-key and multi-key filters work correctly, and filter values are passed as parameterized query values (not interpolated).

Jest unit tests exist for the case-conversion utility covering: simple camelCase (`employeeId`), single-word (`id`), already-snake_case (`employee_id`), multi-segment (`leaveTypeId`), and edge cases (empty string, consecutive capitals like `XMLParser`).

constraints: The utility must be placed in the shared layer (`src/shared/`) — it is a cross-cutting concern, not tied to any single domain module.
The conversion must be applied inside `BaseRepository.findAll()` only — no other repository methods (`findById`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) are in scope for this phase.
The utility must not mutate the original filters object — it must return a new object with converted keys.
Parameterized queries must be preserved — filter values must continue to use `$1`, `$2`, etc. placeholders, never string interpolation.
The utility must be a pure function with no side effects and no external dependencies.
outOfScope: Applying the case conversion to repository methods other than `findAll` (e.g., `create`, `update`, `findById`, or any domain-specific repository methods like `findByEmployeeId`).
Applying the case conversion to any domain service, controller, or route layer — this phase is scoped to the shared base repository only.
Reverse conversion (snake_case to camelCase) for mapping database result rows back to domain objects — only the filter-key direction is in scope.
Handling nested filter objects or array-valued filters — only flat `Record<string, unknown>` filters are in scope.

ambiguities.description: The exact file path for the utility within `src/shared/` is not specified. Candidates include `src/shared/utils/case-conversion.ts`, `src/shared/utils/string.ts`, or a barrel-exported function from `src/shared/utils/index.ts`.

The exact behavior for consecutive uppercase characters (e.g., `XMLParser`, `HTMLElement`) is not specified. Standard camelCase-to-snake_case conventions vary: some produce `xml_parser`, others `xmlparser`.
options: Insert underscore before every uppercase letter: XMLParser → x_m_l_parser
Insert underscore before uppercase letters only at word boundaries: XMLParser → xml_parser
Only handle the domain-relevant patterns (single capital per segment) and treat consecutive capitals as a single word boundary
